channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Solar Farm in Limpopo Boosts Rural Communities' Economy and Green Energy Transition
item.description: A solar farm in Limpopo, South Africa, is showing how renewable energy projects can support rural communities while avoiding land disputes. The 347-hectare Bolobedu solar farm, built on land under cus...

item.title: PCSO Transfers Medical Vehicles to Improve Emergency Health Access in Mimaropa
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) has transferred medical vehicles to local government units (LGUs) in the Mimaropa region to enhance emergency health services. The handover ceremony, h...

item.title: The Kinks' Ray and Dave Davies Reflect on Reconciliation and Possible Reunion
item.description: Ray and Dave Davies of The Kinks have opened up about their improved relationship and potential for a future reunion, following the launch of their joint exhibition 'The Kinks: Brothers' in London. Th...

item.title: Jimothy the Raccoon's Remarkable Survival Despite Disability
item.description: Jimothy, a unique-looking raccoon in Seattle, has become an internet sensation after surviving in the wild despite having a rare cervical vertebral malformation known as short spine syndrome. This con...

item.title: Regina Police Highlight Safety Guidelines for E-Bike and E-Scooter Users
item.description: Regina police have issued a public safety advisory regarding the increasing use of electric bikes and scooters in the city. The warning emphasizes the importance of adhering to traffic laws, wearing a...

item.title: Oracle and Pentagon Enter 10-Year Software Contract
item.description: Oracle has signed a 10-year contract with the U.S. Department of Defense, valued at up to $7 billion, to provide on-premises software solutions, licenses, maintenance, and consulting services to milit...

item.title: Bafana Bafana Player Zwane's Red Card Investigation Reveals Concerns Over Match Integrity
item.description: The red card shown to Themba Zwane during Bafana Bafana's 2026 FIFA World Cup opener against Mexico has sparked controversy, with the Group of Copenhagen flagging it as one of seven 'yellow notices' i...

item.title: Rider killed in multi-vehicle collision in San Ildefonso
item.description: A rider lost their life in a severe multi-vehicle collision that occurred in San Ildefonso, Bulacan. The incident involved six vehicles, including a truck and motorcycle, which collided at a critical ...

item.title: Crafting Art through Handmade Books and International Photography Exhibitions
item.description: The interview highlights the founding of Show LA, a photography fair initiated by three collaborators who drew inspiration from the SF Art Book Fair. The event, held in Los Angeles during Frieze LA, f...

item.title: Nigeria's Army Restructured into 12 Divisions to Enhance National Security
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has approved a major restructuring of the Nigerian Army, expanding its structure from eight to twelve divisions and recruiting 28,000 new personnel to strengthen national defense...

item.title: Canada's Flag-Bearer Kady Dandeneau Leads Team into Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games Opening Ceremony
item.description: The Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games opening ceremony commenced with wheelchair basketball star Kady Dandeneau carrying the Canadian flag and lawn bowler Ryan Bester as the baton-bearer, marking Canada...

item.title: Court Resolves ADC Governorship Dispute, Recognizes Lawal as Ogun Candidate
item.description: A Federal High Court in Abeokuta has resolved a long-standing dispute within the African Democratic Congress (ADC) over the governorship candidacy for Ogun State. The court declared Otunba Jimi Adebis...

item.title: Ten Patriotism Tales by Asghar Wajahat Explore National Love
item.description: The article presents ten short stories by Asghar Wajahat that critically examine patriotism through dialogues and narratives. The tales contrast historical nationalist sacrifices with modern shallow e...

item.title: Xenophobia's Economic Impact and Call for African Unity
item.description: Award-winning stylist and entrepreneur Tracy-Lee Rosslind criticizes xenophobia and its economic consequences for South Africa in a recent interview. Rosslind, who won the Fashion Stylist of the Year ...

item.title: 10 Provinces Recognized for WPS Protection Efforts
item.description: The Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) recently recognized 10 provincial governments as champions of the West Philippine Sea (WPS) during the Bayanihan Para sa Kanlurang Dagat ng P...

item.title: UN Warns of Escalating Middle East Conflict Risk
item.description: United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres has issued a stark warning about the escalating conflict involving the United States, Israel, and Iran, emphasizing that it is pushing the Middle East...

item.title: Mary Trump Criticizes Uncle Donald's Economic Mismanagement and Rally Tactics
item.description: Mary Trump, niece of President Donald Trump, harshly criticized her uncle in her latest column, accusing him of attempting to obscure his mounting failures. She specifically targeted his recent event ...

item.title: News Corp Australia warns of bot detection issues
item.description: News Corp Australia has issued a message indicating that its website employs software to manage traffic from crawler bots. The notice warns that users experiencing access issues may be detected as aut...

item.title: Delhi School Switches to Hybrid Classes Amid Protests and Security Measures
item.description: RS Junior Modern School in New Delhi has shifted to a hybrid mode of education for students from Nursery to Class 5, effective from July 24, 2026, due to ongoing protests and heightened security arran...

item.title: Trump Considers Major Strike on Iran Amid Escalating Tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump is seriously considering a major military strike against Iran, according to Axios. The decision, which could happen within days, would involve operations larger than the 2024...
